arts.ttl: Sean Dyche denies Dominic Calvert-Lewin Everton fight as he admits new Dele Alli blow
arts.sum: Sean Dyche has denied that he had a bust-up with Everton striker Dominic Calvert-Lewin but admitted Dele Alli has suffered another fitness setback

arts.ttl: Newcastle and Everton could be trickier games than City and Madrid, warns Slot
arts.sum: Liverpool face two trips in wake of high-profile victories Slot jokes about charges levelled at Pep Guardiola’s club Arne Slot believes Liverpool’s imperious form has made them a bigger target for Premier League rivals and that trips to Newcastle and Everton could be more problematic than the statement wins against Real Madrid and Manchester City. The Liverpool’s head coach says it was not difficult for his team to show their prowess in beating the champions of Europe last week, and of England at Anfield on Sunday. The challenge now is to repeat those performances at St James’ Park on Wednesday and in Slot’s first Merseyside derby at Goodison Park on Saturday. He says Liverpool’s mentality on the road will be just as important as their ability. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Posh boss Ferguson ponders Young FA Cup date with Everton dad
arts.sum: Peterborough boss Darren Ferguson says he will "have to find a way" to make their FA Cup tie at Everton a family affair for Ashley and Tyler Young.

arts.ttl: Team News: Everton vs. Wolverhampton Wanderers injury, suspension list, predicted XIs
arts.sum: Sports Mole rounds up all of the latest injury and suspension news ahead of Wednesday's Premier League clash between Everton and Wolverhampton Wanderers.

arts.ttl: Everton v Wolves: FPL team news + predicted line-ups
arts.sum: Team form, absentees and our predicted XIs The post Everton v Wolves: FPL team news + predicted line-ups appeared first on Best FPL Tips, Advice, Team News, Picks, and Statistics from Fantasy Football Scout.

arts.ttl: Sean Dyche admits making mistakes in tireless effort to improve Everton form
arts.sum: Manager: ‘The errors I’ve made are tactical’ Dominic Calvert-Lewin was dropped against United Sean Dyche said he is working harder than at any point in his managerial career to correct Everton’s season but has made mistakes in the search for a winning formula. The manager is in desperate need of victory over Wolves on Wednesday after only two league wins all season, no goals in the past four matches and fixtures against Liverpool, Arsenal, Chelsea and Manchester City to follow. He was left to lament an error-strewn performance in the 4-0 defeat at Manchester United on Sunday, but admits his players are not the only ones responsible for lapses this term. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: 5 G/A in 6 starts: Player Everton let go for £3.4m inspiring title charge at new club
arts.sum: He's got three assists in his last two games...

arts.ttl: Wolves need to cut out errors - O'Neil
arts.sum: Wolves need to cut out errors if they are to move away from the bottom of the Premier League, says boss Gary O'Neil.

arts.ttl: I saw a different Sean Dyche today - it's a side his Everton staff have told me about
arts.sum: Chris Beesley takes a look behind the scenes on covering Sean Dyche's pre-match press conference to preview Everton's home game with Wolverhampton Wanderers

arts.ttl: Sean Dyche makes 'error' admission as Everton fight for Premier League survival
arts.sum: Everton face Wolves in a relegation showdown at Goodison Park and Sean Dyche has been frank when addressing what has gone wrong this season
